summary: Iter-61 review of 5 user fixes. Backend: 20/20 pytest pass on test_iteration61_admin_extras.py covering (a) master-query snapshot geo aggregates [therapists_by_state/city/zip all populated, ID present in states, 168+ ID therapists confirmed], (b) master-query NL Q 'How many therapists in Boise?' returns answer with digit + Boise reference, (c) intake-rate-limit GET/PUT incl. boundaries (1/1, 1000/43200 accepted; 0/60, 1001/60, 5/0, 5/43201 all 400), (d) scrape-sources GET/PUT incl. valid 2-row persist with auto-id, invalid url 400 (ftp), missing url 400, >50 400, non-list 400, auth required. Frontend smoke (Playwright): logo-click scroll-to-top FIX VERIFIED — on /therapists/join after scrolling to scrollY=2906, clicking the logo navigates to '/' and lands at scrollY=0 (the bug-of-record was scrollY=3893). Same behavior verified on /admin. Landing page renders hero ('Let therapists come to you.') and header. Universal navbar visible on /admin AND /blog. No console errors during smoke.
action_items: OPTIONAL: Live-test the Admin → More → Settings rate-limit save UI and More → Scrape sources panel UI in browser (I verified the backend contracts they call but did not click through the panels — main agent already smoked them per Iter-61 description). Use admin password admin123! at /admin.

OPTIONAL: Confirm extra_sources_block injection actually appears in outreach_agent + gap_recruiter LLM prompts when sources are configured (the registry endpoints were tested; the prompt-injection path is source-only per agent_to_agent_context_note).
critical_code_review_comments: routes/admin.py is now ~1600 lines and handles login, team mgmt, profile completeness, requests, therapists, declines, backfill, stats, outreach, opt-out HTML, scrape-sources, rate-limit, reviews, coverage-gap analysis. Strongly consider splitting into routes/admin/{team,therapists,outreach,config,settings}.py — the file exceeds the 700-line guideline more than 2x and slows code review.

_coerce_source URL validation accepts any string starting with http:// or https:// — does not run urllib.parse to verify host/path. A user could save 'https:// ' (space) or 'https://x' which won't actually fetch. Cheap fix: urllib.parse.urlparse(url).netloc must be non-empty.

Master-query snapshot zip aggregation iterates EVERY active therapist in Python with a regex over office_addresses on every snapshot rebuild. Fine at 168 docs but should be cached with the rest of the snapshot (it already is, via _SNAPSHOT_TTL_SECS=60 — verified).
Intake-rate-limit endpoint converts the upper-bound check using literal `30 * 24 * 60 = 43200` — magic number repeated in the error message; pull into a module constant.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (4/4 smoke checks: logo scroll on /therapists/join, logo scroll on /admin, landing render, navbar visibility on /admin and /blog)

test_credentials: Admin master pwd: admin123! via X-Admin-Password header. Used for all backend tests.
seed_data_creation: Tests use temporary scrape_sources entries that are reset to [] in teardown_class. Intake rate-limit reset to {1, 60} default in teardown_class. No persistent test data left behind.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Iter-61 backend contracts (master-query geo, intake rate-limit, scrape-sources) are green. Logo scroll-to-top fix in ScrollManager.jsx + SiteShell.jsx verified working on /therapists/join AND /admin via Playwright (scrollY=0 after click, URL='/'). Landing.jsx still renders cleanly (no useFaqs regression). Header/navbar visible on /admin and /blog. NOT TESTED in browser: Admin Settings panel UI save flow, Scrape Sources panel add/save flow, Site Copy preview, FAQ editor up/down arrows (note: iter22 reported reorder route was 404'd by route ordering — main agent should re-verify if Iter-60 reorder route fix is in place). Therapist Edit Profile photo+license fields not browser-tested either. The 168 ID therapists in directory is confirmed via /api/admin/stats and snapshot.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. Prior bugs cited in review_request (scrollY=3893 after logo click; rate-limit 'won't let me change') both verified fixed via the new ScrollManager 3-frame scroll-forcing + widened backend bounds (1-1000 max_per, 1-43200 window, all boundary cases pass).
